items.title: South African, German foreign ministers hold talks in Pretoria
items.description: Germany’s Federal Minister of Foreign Affairs, Dr Johann Wadephul met with South Africa's foreign minister, Ronald Lamola with talks that would focus on growing the economy and accelerating the Just Energy Transition and addressed pressing global challenges

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear deal contingent on Riyadh normalising relations with Israel
items.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday set a new condition to a US-Saudi nuclear deal signed the previous day by insisting the agreement was "totally subject to" the Gulf kingdom joining the Abraham Accords. Riyadh has resisted normalising relations with Israel, especially after the Gaza war, and the new condition throws the US-Saudi deal into turmoil.

items.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.

items.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.description: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.title: US and Saudi Arabia sign new nuclear agreement paving way for kingdom to enrich uranium
items.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia on Wednesday forged a new agreement that could potentially provide the kingdom with uranium enrichment capability for its civilian nuclear program.

items.title: Is this Italy’s ‘George Floyd moment’?
items.description: The death of Moroccan-born Abderrahim Fakir after he was restrained by police in Bologna has sparked protests.

items.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
items.description: This week marks one year since a military jet struck a school in Dhaka, killing 36, most of them children.

items.title: Milei opens door to electric cars as Musk's Tesla firms up arrival plans
items.description: Argentina’s car market is undergoing a silent but fast-moving revolution. While the world's most eccentric mobility brand, Elon Musk’s Telsa, prepares for its arrival in the national market, the landscape has already been transformed by new Asian giants. Leer más

items.title: Morocco defender Issa Diop signs 4-year contract with Ipswich
items.description: Morocco defender Issa Diop returned from the World Cup to switch between Premier League clubs on Wednesday, joining Ipswich from Fulham.

items.title: Why is Trump targeting Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site? Here’s what we know
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran by threatening to bomb the under-construction Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site, located south of Tehran. Why is he targeting it?

items.title: Iran vows to continue strikes in West Asia war
items.description: The armed forces' retaliatory attacks will continue as long as the U.S. attacks on the country's infrastructure and coastal areas continue, says Iran's army spokesperson Mohammad Akraminia

items.title: Nigeria: Armed 'bandits' kill more than 20 in Zamfara state
items.description: Armed gangs, known locally as "bandits," are a constant threat in northwestern and central Nigeria. They typically raid villages, steal livestock and kidnap residents for ransom.

items.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
items.description: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

items.title: Germany seeks energy and trading partners in Africa
items.description: Germany wants to boost relations with Africa. During his trip, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has promoted cooperation on energy, infrastructure and the economy. He also criticized US treatment of South Africa.

items.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
items.description: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

items.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
items.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: African Trade: Bringing AfCFTA Closer to Local Businesses {Business Africa}
items.description: Access to finance also remains a critical obstacle. items.title: Six killed in Algeria forest fires: interior ministry
items.description: Wildfires in Algeria have killed six people, the interior ministry announced, with several fires still raging across the North African country gripped by a heatwave.

items.title: WAFCON 2026: What you need to know
items.description: Several months after it was supposed to start and with more teams than first planned, WAFCON 2026 is finally set to begin. The hosts want a maiden title while a tournament outsider have one of its best players.

items.title: ARA San Juan culpability trial: Plaintiff lawyer says sentences are ‘offensive’
items.description: In an exclusive interview with the Noticias Argentinas news agency, plaintiff lawyer Valeria Carreras says the verdict will be appealed and that the sentence was “a slap in the face.” Leer más

items.title: Messi eyes glorious farewell as Spain, Argentina clash in World Cup final
items.description: Holders Argentina are bidding to become the first team in 64 years to successfully defend the title, in what likely to be the final World Cup match of captain Lionel Messi's career. Leer más

items.title: Germany: Merz replaces key figure following surrogacy row
items.description: Thorsten Frei, a close confidant of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is taking over the role as parliamentary group leader for the conservative CDU/CSU bloc. The position is a powerful one.

items.title: Houthis strike tankers in Red Sea; first since Saudi-linked shipping blockade via Bab el-Mandeb
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump warned on July 22, 2026 that the U.S. would destroy one bridge or power plant each time Iran shoots at a ship in the strait

items.title: Man linked to Isla Bell death fails to have case thrown out
items.description: Marat Ganiev was seeking a permanent stay on his case, with his lawyer arguing the multiple changes to the prosecution case were an abuse of process.

items.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup
items.description: After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.

items.title: Funding cuts threaten Africa's progress in fight against disease
items.description: A growing funding crisis dominated discussions at the African Union's Extraordinary Summit on Health in Accra on Wednesday. Leaders warned that cuts in international aid and limited domestic investment are threatening decades of progress against diseases including HIV, malaria and tuberculosis.

items.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
items.description: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

items.title: Zelenskyy sacks Army Chief Syrsky after days of protests
items.description: The Ukrainian leader announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place.

items.title: Why Iran is targeting Kuwait's desalination plants as part of its war strategy
items.description: Iranian strikes targeted Kuwait’s desalination and power plants for the fourth consecutive day on Monday. The attacks are putting unprecedented pressure on a Gulf country heavily dependant on desalination for its water supply. Iran is attempting to wage a “psychological war” on a country with few options for striking back, according to some analysts.

items.title: China begins two days of live-fire drills in Taiwan Strait
items.description: The news comes a day after ‌talks between U.S. Secretary ‌of State ⁠Marco Rubio and ⁠Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on a range of issues, including ​democratically-governed Taiwan, which China ‌claims as its own territory.

items.title: Moroccans rally outside Casablanca court for release of rapper
items.description: Supporters of Moroccan rapper El Mahdi Lyoubi, known by his stage name "Mehdi Black Wind", gathered outside the Ain Sebaa court in Casablanca on Wednesday to demand his release.

items.title: MotoGP to rev up motorcycle culture and pilgrimages to City of Churches
items.description: The arrival of the Tour Down Under in 1999 inspired a significant uptick in Lycra-wearing cyclists in and around Adelaide. Will the MotoGP have a similar effect for high-performance motorcycles?

items.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
items.description: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.
